The Scroll The Pod

The Foundation

6 lessons · Week 1
01

SQL Fundamentals

From Prisma to raw SQL. CREATE, INSERT, SELECT, WHERE, UPDATE, DELETE. The core vocabulary.
Week 1
02

SELECT & Filtering Deep Dive

Aliases, pattern matching, NULL handling, CASE expressions. Mastering the query.
Week 1
03

JOINs (The Power Move)

INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER, CROSS, self joins. Connecting related tables.
Week 1
04

Aggregation & GROUP BY

COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX. Grouping rows and filtering groups with HAVING.
Week 1
05

Subqueries & CTEs

Queries inside queries. WITH clauses for readable, composable SQL.
Week 1
06

Window Functions

ROW_NUMBER, RANK, LAG, LEAD. SQL's superpower for analytics.
Week 1

Going Deep

6 lessons · Week 2
07

Data Types & Constraints

VARCHAR, INTEGER, JSONB, TIMESTAMP. CHECK, UNIQUE, FOREIGN KEY. Data integrity.
Week 2
08

Indexes & Performance

B-tree, GIN, EXPLAIN ANALYZE. Making queries fast.
Week 2
09

PostgreSQL-Specific Features

JSONB operators, arrays, generate_series, full-text search.
Week 2
10

Database Design & Normalization

1NF, 2NF, 3NF. ERD diagrams. Schema design patterns.
Week 2
11

Advanced Query Patterns

Pivot tables, recursive queries, lateral joins, materialized views.
Week 2
12

Capstone: Real-World Queries

Election data, census analysis, public records. Journalism-ready SQL.
Week 2

Spaces

Practice, build, listen
Reference

Use Cases

Real-world mini-demos: election queries, census analysis, pivot tables, recursive CTEs, and more.

15 live demos
Practice

Games & Quizzes

JOIN visualizer, query builder, schema detective, index optimizer, speed round. Reinforcement through play.

10 games
Portfolio

Interview Projects

Production-quality SQL projects: election database, analytics pipeline, public records explorer, data warehouse.

4 projects
Social

The Scroll

Replace doomscrolling with learning. Interview Qs, hot takes, tips from the dev timeline.

Infinite feed
Audio

The Pod

AI-generated podcast episodes that turn each lesson into conversational audio you can learn from on the go.

12+ episodes

Live Demo: What SQL Looks Like

A taste of what you'll be writing by the end of Week 1.

SELECT name, department, salary
FROM employees
WHERE salary > 75000
ORDER BY salary DESC
LIMIT 6;
namedepartmentsalary
Sarah ChenEngineering142,000
Marcus RiveraEngineering128,000
Priya SharmaData Science118,000
James O'BrienProduct105,000
Aisha JohnsonDesign95,000
David KimMarketing82,000
(6 rows)
